output_summary: aligned schemas for datasets: kaggle_small, uber_eats_small, yelp_small; generated 2 schema correspondence groups.
profiled datasets: kaggle_small, uber_eats_small, yelp_small.
Reused the blocking configuration from state.

Blocking uses `semantic_similarity` for kaggle_small_uber_eats_small, uber_eats_small_yelp_small, blocking on name_norm, city, state; `exact_match_multi` for kaggle_small_yelp_small, blocking on phone_e164, postal_code.
Performance-wise, pair completeness is 1.00 for all pairs and candidate counts range from 179 to 199972.
The config also records dataset id columns.

matcher mode `ml` with config source reused from state; evaluated pairs: id_columns, matching_strategies; selected scores: matching_strategies (threshold=0.72, f1=0.975609756097561, threshold=0.75, f1=1.0); produced 2 pair strategy entries.
This run produced an initial draft of the pipeline.
The pipeline was created to load three restaurant datasets from parquet files and three gold-standard correspondence files from CSV files.
The matching stage was configured pairwise across kaggle_small, uber_eats_small, and yelp_small using a mix of EmbeddingBlocker and StandardBlocker components.

The pipeline added pair-specific feature extraction with string and numeric comparators over fields such as name_norm, city, state, street, postal_code, phone_e164, latitude, longitude, and categories.

The matching stage was changed to a supervised ML workflow that trains one best classifier per dataset pair through GridSearchCV over RandomForest, LogisticRegression, GradientBoosting, and SVM models.
The resulting pairwise correspondences were clustered with MaximumBipartiteMatching, written to CSV files, concatenated, and then passed into a fusion stage.
The fusion stage added attribute fusers for 20 output fields, using longest_string for 19 attributes and union for categories.
The pipeline shifted to an ML-based pairwise matching strategy rather than a rule-only or threshold-driven matching flow.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.

matching_config artifact was rewritten from static matching to ML/entity-fusion config in pipeline.py: added EmbeddingBlocker/StandardBlocker, pair-specific comparators, FeatureExtractor, GridSearchCV over RandomForest/LogisticRegression/GradientBoosting/SVC, MLBasedMatcher, MaximumBipartiteMatching, correspondence CSV writes under output/correspondences, DataFusionEngine debug_file output/data_fusion/debug_fusion_data.jsonl, fusion_data.csv.
This run produced an initial draft of the evaluation script for the current fused output.
The evaluation strategy aligned fused records to gold records by deriving an eval_id from _fusion_sources or falling back to the fused cluster _id before running field-specific comparisons.

The script then assigned comparison functions by field type, using tokenized_match for textual and address-style fields, numeric_tolerance_match for numeric coordinates and ratings, and set_equality_match for detected list-like fields.
Name, normalized name, website, map URL, phone, address, locality, postal, country, and source fields were grouped under tokenized_match.
Latitude, longitude, rating, and rating_count were grouped under numeric_tolerance_match.
Detected list-like non-system columns were grouped under set_equality_match after normalization.
System and identifier columns such as id, _id, eval_id, _eval_cluster_id, and _fusion_confidence were excluded from list-like normalization detection.
The script added _parse_source_ids to parse _fusion_sources from native collections or serialized JSON and Python literal strings.
The script added _detect_gold_prefix to infer the dominant gold ID prefix and prefer matching source IDs with that prefix.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
evaluation attempt counter is 1.
The main problem in this run is downstream fusion logic rather than blocking or pairwise matching.
The fused cluster values are being chosen poorly, especially through generic representative selection and overuse of longest_string.
The run is producing incorrect canonical representatives for key fields such as _id, source, phone fields, and some normalized address and URL fields.
Blocking pair completeness is 1.0 for all dataset pairs.
Matching F1 is 0.976, 1.0, and 0.976 across the dataset pairs, which indicates matching is already strong.
Cluster analysis shows healthy 2-record clusters only, with no one-to-many ambiguity and no oversized clusters.
Overall accuracy is 0.7471839799749687 and macro accuracy is 0.7593984962406016 over 799 evaluated attribute comparisons.
_id_accuracy is 0.275 and source_accuracy is 0.275.
phone_raw_accuracy is 0.0 and phone_e164_accuracy is 0.0.
map_url_accuracy is 0.375, rating_accuracy is 0.675, and street_accuracy is 0.85.
The analysis states that many gold Yelp and Uber records are fused with a Kaggle ID as the final _id.
The analysis states that many expected yelp or uber_eats source values are overwritten by kaggle_380k.
This run produced an initial draft pipeline.

The pipeline added a new fusion strategy that applies longest_string to 19 attributes including name, name_norm, website, map_url, phone_raw, phone_e164, address fields, street, house_number, city, state, postal_code, country, latitude, longitude, rating, rating_count, and source.
The pipeline used union only for the categories field.
The run kept the upstream blocking and ML matching setup strong and unchanged in emphasis, with embedding and standard blockers plus ML-based matchers and maximum bipartite clustering before fusion.
The behavior in this run shifted the system toward generic fusion driven by longest_string rather than source-aware or type-aware representative selection.

The fused representative selection implicitly favored longer raw values, which the evaluation analysis identified as causing Kaggle IDs and source values to overwrite expected Yelp or Uber Eats canonical values.
The run did not introduce any blocking or matching threshold shift, and the main effective behavior change was downstream in fusion.
Singleton retention was disabled because include_singletons was set to False.
No explicit alignment fields were configured for fusion.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.

cluster_analysis_result/history populated; cluster summary artifact written with cluster_summary_path and cluster_summary_run_id; matching_config artifact updated and matching_config_updated=true; analyses reported healthy long-tail clusters with recommended_strategy=None/action=None, e.g. correspondences_kaggle_small_uber_eats_small.csv: total_clusters=124, max_cluster_size=2, small_cluster_ratio=1.0, large_cluster_ratio=0.0, ambiguous_ratio=0.0; node completed ok and left NEXT_NODE=PENDING
This run was a refinement of the evaluation script in evaluation_refinement mode rather than an initial draft or repair.

The dominant strategy was to adapt evaluation functions by field type, replacing tokenized phone evaluation with a custom digit-normalizing phone_match while keeping tokenized matching for text fields, numeric_tolerance_match for numeric fields, and set_equality_match for list-like fields.

The run also strengthened evaluation alignment by preserving the eval-view expansion from _fusion_sources and explicitly registering _id for evaluation when both fused and gold identifiers were available.

Name, provenance, website, map URL, and address text fields were grouped under tokenized_match through an explicit tokenized_columns list covering name, name_norm, source, website, map_url, address_line1, address_line2, street, house_number, city, state, postal_code, and country.
Phone fields were split into their own group and evaluated with a custom phone_match function for phone_raw and phone_e164 instead of tokenized_match.
Numeric fields were grouped under numeric_tolerance_match for latitude, longitude, rating, and rating_count.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 72.59%; evaluation attempt counter is 2.
The main failure in this run is incorrect fused attribute selection inside matched clusters rather than blocking or pairwise matching quality.
The fusion strategy is overusing longest_string across many fields, which is producing wrong canonical values, wrong source assignment, and wrong record identity.
A secondary problem is missing fused output coverage for some records, likely due to singleton exclusion and evaluation mapping dependence on _fusion_sources.
A tertiary problem is datatype and normalization issues in phone and numeric-like fields.
Overall accuracy is 0.7259073842302879 across 799 evaluations with 580 correct results.
Source accuracy is 0.275, _id accuracy is 0.275, and map_url accuracy is 0.3, which is consistent with wrong canonical record selection during fusion.
Phone_e164 accuracy is 0.0 and phone_raw accuracy is 0.0, with examples such as 16024284444.0 versus 16024284444 indicating representation problems.
The analysis reports that source is almost always fused as kaggle_380k even when the expected source is yelp or uber_eats.
The analysis reports that website is sometimes taken from the wrong restaurant in the cluster.
This run was an initial draft of the pipeline.
The pipeline added a new fusion stage that concatenates the three pairwise correspondence outputs and applies a DataFusionStrategy over the combined correspondences.
The fusion policy assigned longest_string to 19 attributes, including identity-like, address, phone, URL, source, and numeric fields, while categories used union.
The run kept the upstream blocking and matching setup and introduced pairwise MaximumBipartiteMatching clustering before fusion.
The pipeline shifted to a fusion behavior dominated by longest_string selection rather than field-specific or provenance-aware resolution.
The fused output is built from combined pairwise bipartite matches across all dataset pairs instead of using an explicit canonical-record or source-priority policy.
The run excluded standalone entities from the fused output because singleton retention was disabled.

The added attribute fusers covered name, name_norm, website, map_url, phone_raw, phone_e164, address_line1, address_line2, street, house_number, city, state, postal_code, country, latitude, longitude, rating, rating_count, and source with longest_string.
Categories was the only field using a non-dominant helper, namely union.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.

cluster_analysis_result added health reports for correspondence files including correspondences_kaggle_small_uber_eats_small.csv with total_clusters=124, max_cluster_size=2, small_cluster_ratio=1.0, one_to_many_ratio=0.0, ambiguous_ratio=0.0, score mean/p50/p75/p90=1.0 and meets_target/is_healthy=true, and correspondences_uber_eats_small_yelp_small.csv with analogous healthy metrics for 111 clusters; cluster_summary_path/run_id/written and matching_config_updated were set.
This run was an evaluation refinement rather than an initial draft or repair.
The dominant strategy was to refine evaluation alignment and comparators so measured errors better reflected fusion-stage attribute selection failures instead of ID-mapping or datatype artifacts.

Identity and source-like fields such as _id and source were evaluated as tokenized text, while gold-ID alignment was refined by mapping eval_id from _fusion_sources using prefix detection that now supports hyphenated IDs.
Text and address-oriented fields including name, name_norm, address_line1, address_line2, street, city, state, postal_code, country, website, and map_url were grouped under tokenized_match.
Phone fields phone_e164 and phone_raw were grouped under a custom phone_match that strips formatting noise and trailing .0 artifacts before digit comparison.
Numeric fields latitude, longitude, rating, and rating_count were grouped under numeric_tolerance_match.
List-like fields including categories and any detected normalized list columns were grouped under set_equality_match, while house_number was separated to exact_string_match.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 57.07%; previous logged accuracy was 72.59%; evaluation attempt counter is 3.
Run results were persisted for reproducibility and offline analysis.
